spec_match.rationale: The feature exposes dataTransfer on the `input` event for insertFromPaste/insertFromDrop/insertReplacementText. This page listens on the `beforeinput` event — which already carried dataTransfer pre-v143. So the page will report the dataTransfer payload identically on Chrome 142 and Chrome 143, demonstrating nothing about the v143 change. The chromestatus blurb on the feature index even says 'extends that capability to the corresponding input events' — and yet every script here is on beforeinput.

interactive.rationale: Contenteditable, real paste/drop/replacement triggers, structured log, and reset/clear controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says 'paste, drop, or use spell-check replacement … shows the live beforeinput event with its inputType and the items inside event.dataTransfer'. The page does that — but the v143 feature is about the `input` event, not beforeinput. Blurb and page agree only because they both miss the spec.

edge_cases.rationale: Detects InputEvent.prototype.dataTransfer presence (good idea); logs item types and the first 80 chars of text/plain. Doesn't show the pre- vs post-v143 contrast that the chromestatus summary calls out: beforeinput already had it, input did not.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6715253274181632 and the input-events spec.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colour palette; warn/ok/pill classes use design-system colours.

openQuestions.title: Add an `input` event listener alongside the beforeinput one
openQuestions.detail: The v143 feature is `InputEvent.dataTransfer` on the **input** event. Add a parallel listener on `editor.addEventListener('input', …)` and log the dataTransfer payload from there. Side-by-side, the visitor will see: beforeinput → already had it; input → null on Chrome 142, populated on Chrome 143. That's the actual demo.

openQuestions.title: Add a pre-vs-post-v143 sentinel readout
openQuestions.detail: Once both events are listened to, surface a top-of-page badge: 'input.dataTransfer for insertFromPaste: present / absent' so a visitor immediately knows whether their browser has the v143 feature without needing to trigger an event.

openQuestions.title: Compare contenteditable vs textarea behaviour
openQuestions.detail: The spec explicitly says the new behaviour is contenteditable-only; on form controls (input/textarea), dataTransfer remains null on input events. A second editor pane with `<textarea>` would teach the scope limit.

openQuestions.title: Cover all three inputTypes in the support probe
openQuestions.detail: `'dataTransfer' in InputEvent.prototype` only tells you the getter exists. Trigger a synthetic paste/drop/replacement event with `new InputEvent('input', { inputType: 'insertFromPaste', dataTransfer: ... })` to probe whether the *populated* value is actually delivered on input vs beforeinput.

summary: Significant spec_match issue: the page listens on `beforeinput` but the v143 feature is specifically about exposing dataTransfer on the `input` event. Adding an `input` listener alongside the existing beforeinput one is the structural fix — and it makes the page actually demonstrate what shipped in v143.
